content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

April 29, 1991

Wallace A. Lagorio, Chairman
Public Relations Counsel
661 Newcastle Road
Newcastle, CA 95658

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-91-185

Dear Mr. Lagorio:

You are seeking general advice on behalf of the Yuba County
Water Agency regarding the agency's duties and responsibilities
under the "mass mailing" provision, Section 89001, of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 We provide you with advice as
the agency's authorized representative, pursuant to Regulation
18329 (copy enclosed).

The following advice is based upon the facts provided in your
letter of March 19, 1991 and in a telephone conversation on
April 2, 1991. Because of a forthcoming publication deadline we
provided you with telephone advice on April 2, and this letter
will serve to confirm that advice.

QUESTION

Can the Yuba County Water Agency include in its quarterly
newsletter, inserted in a local newspaper for distribution, a
masthead which includes the names of the elected directors of the
agency?

CONCLUSION

The Yuba County Water Agency is permitted to include the
names of its elected directors in a masthead or roster listing
that appears in its quarterly newsletter. The placement of the

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

File No. A-90-185
Page 2

masthead or roster listing must be in an appropriate location in
the newsletter.

FACTS

Included in the work you undertake at the Yuba County Water
Agency ("the agency") is the publication of an agency quarterly
newsletter. You wish to include in the forthcoming newsletter a
listing of the names of the agency's elected directors that would
appear in a masthead or other type of roster listing in the
newsletter, a copy of which was included with your letter. The
newsletter will be included in the Marysville-Yuba City Appeal-
Democrat, a local newspaper, for distribution. At present, you
are uncertain where the masthead or roster listing would actually
be placed in the newsletter.

ANALYSIS

Section 82041.5 defines a mass mailing as more than 200
substantially similar pieces of mail. Section 89001 prohibits a
newsletter or other mass mailing from being sent at public
expense.

Regulation 18901 (copy enclosed) implements Section 89001 and
clarifies that the purpose of the mass mailing restriction is to
prevent elected officers from using public funds to further their
candidacy for reelection by sending out newsletters and other mass
mailings. (Krannawitter Advice Letter, A-89-300, Symkowick Advice
Letter, I-88-399, copies enclosed.)

Affiliation

Regulation 18901 provides that a mass mailing may not be sent
within the meaning of Section 89001 if, among other things, the
item sent either features an elected officer affiliated with the
agency which produces or sends the mailing (Regulation
18901(a)(2)(A)), or includes the name or other reference to an
elected officer affiliated with the agency which produces or sends
the mailing, which is prepared in consultation with the elected
officer. (Regulation 18901(a)(2)(B).)

The mailing falls within the mass mailing restrictions when
the elected official is affiliated with the agency that sends the
mailing. An elected officer is affiliated with an agency when he
or she is, among other things, an officer of the agency.
(Regulation 18901(c)(1).)

Because the directors of the agency are elected, they are
"affiliated" with the agency sending the mailing. Inclusion of
the officials' names would constitute "featuring". (Regulation
18901(c)(2).) Therefore, assuming the other requirements of
Regulation 18901(a) have been met, the newsletter could not be

File No. A-90-185
Page 3

sent at public expense unless one of the exceptions is applicable.
As you were informed by telephone, we believe an exception does
apply based on the facts particular to the situation raised by
your inquiry.

Exceptions

Regulation 18901(b) lists several exceptions to the
prohibition of Section 89001. Two exceptions contained in
Regulation 18901(b)(1) appear applicable to your query.

Regulation 18901(b)(1) permits elected officers' names to
appear in a mailing provided it does so only in a letterhead or
logotype of the agency's stationary and forms. It also permits
the names to appear in a roster listing when the names appear in
the same typeface, type size, type color, and location.

A roster that contains the names of the elected district
directors in the same typeface, size and location as the
district's executive officers is in compliance with the mass
mailing provisions of the Act. (Steffen Advice Letter, A-90-033;
Biondo Advice Letter, A-89-022, copies enclosed.)

Although you provided a copy of the masthead intended for
inclusion in the newsletter, you were unable to indicate
specifically where in the newsletter the masthead would be
located. You were informed that our advice with respect to the
listing of the elected directors' names was premised on the
understanding that the inclusion of the masthead or roster listing
was placed in the newsletter in a traditional location, such as
the top or bottom of the front or back pages.

I trust this letter has provided you with the guidance you
requested. If you have any further questions regarding this
matter please contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: Jonathan S. Rothman
Counsel, Legal Division

SH:JSR:ken

Enclosures

sections.analysis:
Section 82041.5 defines a mass mailing as more than 200 substantially similar pieces of mail. Section 89001 prohibits a newsletter or other mass mailing from being sent at public expense.

Regulation 18901 (copy enclosed) implements Section 89001 and clarifies that the purpose of the mass mailing restriction is to prevent elected officers from using public funds to further their candidacy for reelection by sending out newsletters and other mass mailings. (Krannawitter Advice Letter, A-89-300, Symkowick Advice Letter, I-88-399, copies enclosed.)

Affiliation

Regulation 18901 provides that a mass mailing may not be sent within the meaning of Section 89001 if, among other things, the item sent either features an elected officer affiliated with the agency which produces or sends the mailing (Regulation 18901(a)(2)(A)), or includes the name or other reference to an elected officer affiliated with the agency which produces or sends the mailing, which is prepared in consultation with the elected officer. (Regulation 18901(a)(2)(B).)

The mailing falls within the mass mailing restrictions when the elected official is affiliated with the agency that sends the mailing. An elected officer is affiliated with an agency when he or she is, among other things, an officer of the agency. (Regulation 18901(c)(1).)

Because the directors of the agency are elected, they are "affiliated" with the agency sending the mailing. Inclusion of the officials' names would constitute "featuring". (Regulation 18901(c)(2).) Therefore, assuming the other requirements of Regulation 18901(a) have been met, the newsletter could not be

sent at public expense unless one of the exceptions is applicable. As you were informed by telephone, we believe an exception does apply based on the facts particular to the situation raised by your inquiry.

Exceptions

Regulation 18901(b) lists several exceptions to the prohibition of Section 89001. Two exceptions contained in Regulation 18901(b)(1) appear applicable to your query.

Regulation 18901(b)(1) permits elected officers’ names to appear in a mailing provided it does so only in a letterhead or logotype of the agency’s stationary and forms. It also permits the names to appear in a roster listing when the names appear in the same typeface, type size, type color, and location.

A roster that contains the names of the elected district directors in the same typeface, size and location as the District’s executive officers is in compliance with the mass mailing provisions of the Act. (Steffen Advice Letter, A-90-033; Biondo Advice Letter, A-89-022, copies enclosed.)

Although you provided a copy of the masthead intended for inclusion in the newsletter, you were unable to indicate specifically where in the newsletter the masthead would be located. You were informed that our advice with respect to the listing of the elected directors’ names was premised on the understanding that the inclusion of the masthead or roster listing was placed in the newsletter in a traditional location, such as the top or bottom of the front or back pages.

I trust this letter has provided you with the guidance you requested. If you have any further questions regarding this matter please contact me at (916) 322-5901.
